Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta Slashes Domestic Boeing 767-400 Flights By 67% In Massive Schedule Shakeup

The airline will redeploy a significant portion of its 767-400ER fleet from domestic routes to services to and from Europe, affecting several routes.

Delta's redeployment of 767-400ERs to transatlantic routes signals confidence in long-haul profitability while acknowledging domestic market pressures requiring smaller, more efficient aircraft.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Inside The F-35's Cockpit System

At the controls of flying supercomputer.

The F-35's integrated avionics architecture consolidates sensor fusion and weapon systems into a single cockpit interface, fundamentally shifting pilot workload from data management to decision-making.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

TSA Employees Asked To Return Tyler Perry’s $1,000 Gift Cards As Backpay Begins

TSA workers receiving backpay as shutdown enters day 45, but those who received $1,000 gift cards from Tyler Perry are asked to return them.

TSA's gift card clawback during backpay distribution highlights payroll complexity during extended shutdowns and raises questions about employee benefit administration protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

London Gatwick Confirms 9th New Airline In Massive 2026 Shakeup

Pegasus gives Gatwick a fresh Istanbul link as its surprising 2026 growth surges.

Gatwick's nine new airlines in 2026 signal recovery momentum post-capacity constraints, with Turkish carriers particularly aggressive in capturing underserved European routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

The World’s Largest Air Forces By Number Of F-35s

A closer look at some of the leading F-35 armadas around the world.

The F-35's global proliferation across allied nations reflects a strategic shift toward standardized fighter fleets that enhance interoperability while consolidating supply chain dependencies.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document why specific risk controls were chosen over alternatives and justify acceptance of remaining risks to demonstrate regulatory compliance under FAA safety management standards.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal

Decision clears path for April closing after months of scrutiny over keeping key space technology in domestic hands...

Germany's approval removes regulatory barriers for Rocket Lab's acquisition of Mynaric, potentially consolidating critical satellite communications capabilities within a U.S.-backed aerospace entity.

Military Mar 30, 2026

5 Military Aircraft With The Longest Range In Service

Some military aircraft are built for range and endurance, while others only have a long range when they are in ferry configurations.

Ferry configurations can extend effective range significantly, making aircraft versatility critical for military operational planning beyond their standard combat range specifications.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Video: Delta A330 returns to São Paulo after engine failure on takeoff

A Delta Air Lines Airbus A330-300 returned to São Paulo Guarulhos Airport shortly after takeoff on March 29,…...

A330 engine failures during takeoff remain a critical safety concern, requiring immediate investigation into whether this incident reflects systemic maintenance or design issues affecting wide-body fleet reliability.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Airlines Don’t Always Use Their Newest Jets

Examining why the three US legacy carriers operate relatively old aircraft, and why American Airlines' fleet is yoinger than the rest.

Legacy carriers balance depreciation schedules and maintenance costs against fuel efficiency gains, making older aircraft economically rational despite higher operating expenses.

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

A robust reporting culture within Safety Management Systems requires psychological safety and organizational commitment, not just compliance procedures, to effectively identify and mitigate emerging risks.

Artemis II by the numbers: an inside look at mankind’s planned return to the Moon

NASA’s Artemis II astronauts are go for launch, with the agency targeting April 1, 2026, at 18:24 local…...

Artemis II's 2026 timeline requires sustained supply chain coordination across hundreds of contractors, making on-time delivery dependent on workforce stability and component availability beyond NASA's direct control.

Military Mar 30, 2026

China’s Y-9FQ Next Gen ASW Aircraft Intercepted for the First Time Over East China Sea

The Y-9FQ ‘High New 15’ made its debut at the Sep. 3, 2025, Victory Day parade in Beijing, and has now been intercepted for the first time by Japan. A Chinese People’s Liberation Army Naval Air Force (PLANAF) Y-9FQ maritime patrol and anti-submarine warfare aircraft was intercepted for the first...

China's newly revealed Y-9FQ ASW aircraft demonstrates accelerated operational deployment in contested airspace, signaling expanded maritime surveillance capabilities in the East China Sea.

See The Aviationist →

Corporate Mar 30, 2026

Air Canada CEO Announces Retirement Following Criticism Over LaGuardia Crash Comments

Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau's retirement announcement comes after public backlash over remarks perceived as insensitive to a LaGuardia accident.

CEO departures due to reputational damage underscore how crisis communication failures can escalate stakeholder pressure beyond operational performance issues.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Why Pilots Sometimes Land Hard On Purpose

A harder landing often feels dramatic, but it is frequently the safer landing.

Steeper descent angles and firmer landings reduce float distance, enabling safer touchdowns on short or contaminated runways where overshooting poses greater risk.

See Simple Flying →

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures require pilots to navigate complex terrain and SFO Class B airspace simultaneously, demanding heightened situational awareness and procedural compliance.

Some airline routes are special because of the insatiable passenger demand they create, like Seoul to Jeju in South Korea, which is often ranked as the world’s busiest route. Others are special because they link two globally influential cities like New York and London. Then there’s Dallas to New...

Airlines are differentiating through culinary partnerships that reflect regional identity, turning catering into a competitive advantage on high-frequency routes.

NORAD Escorts Aircraft After Palm Beach TFR Violation

F-16s intercepted the general aviation aircraft near Mar-a-Lago on Sunday afternoon.

Sendoff for Artemis II Crew

From left to right, NASA astronauts Andre Douglas, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronauts Jenni Gibbons, NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew proceed to a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas...

Artemis II's international crew composition signals NASA's strategic reliance on Canadian partnership for sustained lunar exploration capabilities.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain disproportionately lethal for helicopters because pilots systematically underestimate detection difficulty and reaction time requirements in low-altitude operations.

Only 27% Full: Avelo Airlines' 10 Emptiest Routes Revealed

Discover the shocking low load factors on Avelo Airlines' emptiest routes, but there's a twist...

Avelo's severely underutilized routes reveal vulnerability in point-to-point regional service models where demand forecasting miscalculations can rapidly erode unit economics.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Air Algérie orders 10 Boeing 737-8s as fleet renewal accelerates

Air Algérie has ordered 10 Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft, with deliveries set to begin in summer 2026 as the…...

North African carriers are increasingly adopting MAX aircraft despite regional hesitation, signaling growing confidence in Boeing's safety record and competitive positioning against Airbus in emerging markets.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer

MILAN — The European Space Agency has launched the first two satellites of the Celeste in-orbit demonstration mission from New Zealand aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher on March 28. The two satellites were launched at 10:14 CET and separated from the launcher about an hour later into a...

Europe's Celeste constellation offers aviation operators a backup GPS layer independent from U.S.-controlled systems, reducing navigation vulnerabilities for commercial and military aircraft operations.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission

SpaceX launched the latest in its series of dedicated rideshare missions March 30, delivering more than 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

SpaceX's rideshare model continues to democratize space access by enabling over 100 small operators to reach orbit simultaneously at reduced cost per payload.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations presents the most viable near-term pathway to accommodate growing demand while reducing aviation's environmental footprint.

See MDPI →

Formation flying with a friend's Subaru-powered home built.

Experimental aircraft with automotive engines remain popular among builders due to lower acquisition costs and proven reliability compared to traditional aircraft powerplants.

eVTOL Mar 30, 2026

Merlin Labs sees incremental path to pilot-free passenger flights

US start-up Merlin Labs is eyeing civil applications for its AI-powered, defense-grade Merlin Pilot autonomous flight control system and says there is “no hard ceiling” for the size of aircraft its technology could eventually be installed on. Describing fully uncrewed civil flight as “a very long...

Merlin Labs' defense-grade autonomous system applicability to larger aircraft could accelerate regulatory acceptance of uncrewed passenger operations beyond current experimental constraints.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Bars U.S. Iran-Related Military Flights From Its Airspace

Madrid expands earlier restrictions on base access for U.S. military flights.

Spain's escalating restrictions on U.S. military flights signal how geopolitical tensions can fragment critical NATO airspace corridors used for transatlantic operations.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit

Chinese firm CAS Space successfully reached orbit with the first launch of its Kinetica-2 launch vehicle Monday, with a prototype cargo spacecraft among the payloads.

China's successful Kinetica-2 debut expands competitive reusable launch options in Asia, potentially pressuring Western providers on cost and frequency.

Press Release: The New Terminal One at JFK selects PRA services vendor

The New Terminal One at JFK has selected Wilson James, a leading provider of Passengers Requiring Assistance (PRA) services, as its dedicated partner to design and operate a comprehensive assistance program for passengers who require additional support when traveling through the new...

JFK's new terminal is outsourcing disability services to a specialized vendor, signaling a shift toward dedicated accessibility partnerships in next-generation airport infrastructure.

FBI says there is no continuing threat after passenger’s verbal bomb-threat.
